description: Fix all on-disk file format differences that prevent the Rust CLI from reading files written by the Go CLI and vice versa. Rename file constants, restructure ForkState/ForkEntry types to match Go's schema, and add a migration that transparently renames old files.

userStories.title: Rename Registry File Constants
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Rust CLI to use the same registry filenames as the Go CLI so that both tools can read each other's files without conflicts.
acceptanceCriteria: SAFE_TXS_FILE constant equals "safe-txs.json"
GOVERNOR_PROPOSALS_FILE constant equals "governor-txs.json"
FORK_STATE_FILE constant equals "fork.json"
grep -r for old filenames (safe_txs.json, governor_proposals.json, fork-state.json) returns zero matches in crates/ (excluding migration code)
All test fixtures and golden files reference the new filenames
cargo check --workspace passes with no errors

userStories.title: Restructure ForkState Top-Level Schema
userStories.description: As a developer, I want ForkState to serialize with a top-level 'forks' key instead of 'activeForks' so that it matches Go's ForkState schema.
acceptanceCriteria: ForkState serializes with top-level keys 'forks' and 'history'
Existing serde round-trip tests pass with the new field name
All ForkStateStore methods compile and function correctly
Fork CLI commands (enter, exit, status, revert, restart, history, diff) compile without errors
cargo check --workspace passes with no errors

userStories.title: Restructure ForkEntry to Match Go Schema
userStories.description: As a developer, I want ForkEntry to include all Go-compatible fields and replace the single evmSnapshotId with a snapshots array so that fork.json is structurally compatible with Go's schema.
acceptanceCriteria: ForkEntry includes Go-compatible fields: envVarName, originalRpc, anvilPid, pidFile, logFile, enteredAt, snapshots
SnapshotEntry struct exists with fields: index, snapshotId, command, timestamp
evm_snapshot_id field is removed from ForkEntry
Fork enter/exit/revert/restart commands populate and use the new fields correctly
Serde round-trip test proves ForkEntry serializes with Go-compatible JSON keys
cargo check --workspace passes with no errors

userStories.title: Verify Config and Registry Schema Compatibility
userStories.description: As a developer, I want config.local.json and registry.json schemas verified against Go's schemas so that any discrepancies are documented or fixed.
acceptanceCriteria: config.local.json field names confirmed matching Go (or fixed if not)
registry.json schema discrepancy documented in code comment with chosen resolution
If resolution requires code changes, those changes are implemented and tested
cargo check --workspace passes with no errors

userStories.title: Add File Rename Migration
userStories.description: As an existing Rust CLI user, I want old-format files to be automatically renamed to the new format on first load so that I experience a seamless upgrade.
acceptanceCriteria: REGISTRY_VERSION equals 2
MIGRATIONS array contains entry for v1 to v2
Migration renames safe_txs.json to safe-txs.json when old file exists
Migration renames governor_proposals.json to governor-txs.json when old file exists
Migration renames fork-state.json to fork.json when old file exists
Migration skips rename if new file already exists (no clobber)
Migration handles fork snapshot directories
Timestamped backup is created before migration runs
Unit test verifies migration renames files correctly
cargo test --workspace passes with all existing and new tests green
